title: Inconsistent source/destination syntax between `copy` and `sync` commands
body:
I have noticed a discrepancy in the syntax requirements between the `skopeo copy` and `skopeo sync` commands, which leads to some confusion. Specifically:  

- When using `skopeo copy`, the `docker://` prefix is required for both the source and destination registries:  
  ```  
  skopeo copy docker://src-registry/image:tag docker://dest-registry/image:tag  
  ```  

- However, with `skopeo sync`, the syntax is different. Instead of `docker://`, the source and destination must be specified using flags such as `--src` and `--dest` combined with `docker`:  
  ```  
  skopeo sync --src docker --dest docker src-registry/image:tag dest-registry/image:tag  
  ```  

#### Question  
Why is the syntax different between these two commands? Wouldn’t it be more consistent to either:  
1. Allow the `--src` and `--dest` flags for `skopeo copy`, or  
2. Enable `docker://` prefixes in `skopeo sync`?  

This inconsistency makes it less intuitive to use the commands together, especially when switching between `copy` and `sync`. Could you clarify the reasoning behind this design decision? Is there a plan to unify the syntax in future versions?

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Pragmatically, the CLI exists that way and we can’t break it.

Specific reasons:
- The `docker://…` and `dir:…` syntax refers, both in `skopeo copy`, `skopeo inspect`, `podman`, and other tools using the containers/image library, to a _single image_. It’s not at all obvious that using the same syntax to refer to a _set of images_ (a repository/directory to sync) would be any less confusing. (Also, there are corner cases. If `docker://busybox` is the same as `docker://docker.io/library/busybox`, would `docker://` be the same as `docker://docker.io/library`??)
- There is `skopeo sync --src yaml`, while there is no `skopeo copy yaml:…`

The two are just not _that_ similar.

I would, in principle, like to improve how these tools deal with a “container of images”, but it’s very unclear that it can be done _in a generic way_; the various “containers” we encounter just don’t have that much in common. Consider how for `containers-storage:`, users would almost certainly want to list _all_ images available locally; but for `docker://`, it must be just a single repo (because the registry protocol, for good reasons, does not have a “list all repos” operation). And how per-image directories with `dir:` can be arranged in arbitrarily-nested hierarchies (with symbolic and hard links…).

WRT specific plans, the primary issue with `sync` is the hard-coded `--scoped=true`/`--scoped=false` set of behaviors. Several tickets here are already tracking redesigning that to make things more generic, but it’s unclear when that could happen.

Thanks a lot for the detailed explanation—it makes much more sense now! I can see how tricky it must be to balance all the edge cases and maintain consistency across the tools.

Really appreciate the work you put into Skopeo. Thanks again for taking the time to respond!
